title: Critical Attack Tree fragment
description: Data-driven attack-tree section rendered as the unnumbered `## Critical Attack Tree` block between the Management Summary and Section 1. The renderer produces the Mermaid graph block (vertical, top-down), the 'Key takeaway' paragraph, and the Stage/Finding/Mitigation reference table. Replaces the dormant critical-attack-chain.schema.json (2026-05 hybrid migration — §3.1 Attack Chain Overview retains chain semantics).

intro.description: One-paragraph intro describing the attacker goal at the root and how to read the tree top-down.

root_goal.description: Short business-impact statement that names the attacker's terminal objective (e.g. 'Full admin takeover', 'Customer PII exfiltration'). Rendered as the root node label.

orientation.description: Trees render top-down. `LR` is forbidden — that layout belongs to §3.1 chain semantics.

class.description: Tree-shape classes: `goal` (root), `and_node` / `or_node` (internal refinement), `leaf` (primitive finding ref). Legacy chain classes are retained for backwards compatibility during the hybrid transition.

finding_ref.description: Optional F-NNN or T-NNN backlink. Required when `class` is `leaf` so the renderer can emit a deep link into §8 Threat Register.

refinement.description: Optional AND/OR semantic annotation. AND = all children required; OR = any child suffices. The renderer surfaces this on the edge label so reviewers can read the tree without re-deriving the boolean structure.

key_takeaway.description: Paragraph beginning with '**Key takeaway:**'. The renderer auto-prepends that prefix; supply only the content.

mitigation_breakpoints.description: Optional list of mitigations (M-NNN) whose deployment severs at least one subtree. Rendered as a sub-bullet list under the key takeaway when non-empty.

stages.description: Rows of the Stage/Finding/Mitigation reference table — the linear walk through the most-likely path from root to leaves, kept for quick-reference parity with the prior chain layout.
